title: AgentManifest
description: AITP v0.1 Agent Manifest — the signed self-description published by every AITP agent. Enables peer agents to discover handshake endpoints, verify identity, and screen trust anchors before initiating a Mutual Handshake (RFC-AITP-0004).

display_name.description: Human-readable agent name. Not used in trust decisions.

handshake_endpoint.description: HTTPS URL where peer agents initiate the Mutual Handshake. MUST use HTTPS.

accepted_trust_anchors.description: OIDC issuer URIs this agent accepts from peers. Peer's identity issuer MUST appear here for handshake to proceed.

offered_capabilities.description: Capabilities this agent is willing to grant to authenticated peers.

required_peer_capabilities.description: Capabilities the peer MUST hold for this agent to accept a handshake. Empty means no prerequisites.

accepted_identity_types.description: Identity binding types this agent accepts from peers (RFC-AITP-0002). Default semantics per RFC-AITP-0003 §3.2: an absent field defaults to ["oidc"]; an explicitly empty [] means 'accept no identity type' (rejects every peer with INCOMPATIBLE_IDENTITY_TYPE — distinct from INCOMPATIBLE_TRUST_ANCHORS, which signals an OIDC trust-anchor overlap failure). Publishers SHOULD omit the field when the default is desired.

accepted_signature_algorithms.description: Signature algorithms this agent accepts from peers (RFC-AITP-0001 §5.4.3). Default semantics per RFC-AITP-0003 §3.2: absent defaults to the mandatory algorithm set for the Manifest's `version` — ["ed25519"] for aitp/0.1 (Ed25519-only per RFC-AITP-0009 §4), ["ed25519", "p256"] for aitp/0.2. An explicit [] rejects every peer's envelope signature. Preserve absent vs explicit-empty in the JCS signing bytes.

challenge.description: Random 128-bit value (base64url) chosen at publish time.

signature.description: base64url(sign(agent_private_key, sha256(base64url_decode(challenge)))). Verified using the public key in `aid`. The hash input is the 16 raw bytes obtained by decoding `challenge`, not the ASCII bytes of the encoded form, matching the convention in RFC-AITP-0004 §3.

published_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when this Manifest was signed.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) after which this Manifest MUST NOT be used.

extensions.description: Reserved for future extension fields. Unknown extensions MUST be ignored.

signature.description: base64url signature over canonical Manifest JSON (excluding this field, keys sorted lexicographically). Signed by the agent's private key.

IdentityHint.description: Static identity metadata published in the Agent Manifest. Contains no verifiable JWT — the verifiable proof is exchanged inline in the Mutual Handshake (RFC-AITP-0004 §3) where it can be bound to a fresh nonce and the verifying peer's AID.

subject.description: Agent subject identifier at the issuer.

public_key.description: base64url public key. Required for pinned_key.
